summary: The backend platform-scoping pipeline (Sprint 10 _resolve_platform_only_scoping + warehouse adapter) is well-exercised, but three open risks remain: (R1) no front-end selector guard drops stale cross-platform store rows between route changes; (R3) demo, fake, and upload adapters silently ignore client_scoped_* keys so they always return unscoped payloads; and (R8) the X-Adinsights-Resolved-Via header is never consumed or parsed by any frontend code, leaving contract regressions invisible.

evidence.note: Function exists and is invoked at line 229; correct prefix rules for meta/ and google-ads.

evidence.snippet: useEffect(() => { if (!routePlatformScope) { return; } ... setFilters({ ...filters, platforms: [...routePlatformScope] }); }, [filters, routePlatformScope, setFilters]);
evidence.note: Effect rewrites filters.platforms on route change — fires AFTER the child page renders, so one render cycle with stale data is possible.
Row-level filter inside store selectors is for channels/campaigns, NOT for platform. No platform-guard drops stale cross-platform rows before fetch completes.
URL-sync effect runs in parallel with the platform-scope effect; ordering is not guaranteed, creating a potential double-write race.

findings.mitigation: Add a per-row selector guard in useDashboardStore selectors (campaign.data, creative.data, etc.) that drops rows where row.platform is not in filters.platforms, OR clear those slices synchronously inside resolveRoutePlatformScope when the scope changes. Add a DashboardLayout test asserting no stale-platform rows appear before the first re-fetch.

evidence.note: DashboardLayout is the intended sole writer to filters.platforms, consistent with the plan.

evidence.snippet: useEffect(() => { if (isSavedDashboardRoute) { return; } ... setFilters(urlFilters); }, [isSavedDashboardRoute, setFilters, urlFilters]);
evidence.note: Second effect also calls setFilters from URL. If URL contains a platforms param the URL-sync fires and may conflict with platform-scope effect, creating a two-effect race on mount.
SavedDashboardPage writes to filters directly (via useDashboardStore.setFilters); DashboardLayout skips URL sync for saved routes but the platform-scope effect still fires.
useMetaStore has its own setFilters that writes to MetaFilters (accountId, since, until, etc.) — does NOT write to useDashboardStore.filters.platforms, so no cross-store conflict.

findings.mitigation: Merge the two setFilters effects in DashboardLayout into one effect that applies routePlatformScope then URL params in a single pass. Add a Vitest that mounts DashboardLayout at /dashboards/meta/accounts with a stale platforms=google_ads URL param and asserts setFilters is called exactly once with platforms=['meta_ads'].

evidence.note: Only warehouse adapter reads and applies client_scoped_* keys. All other adapters silently ignore them.

evidence.snippet: def fetch_metrics(self, *, tenant_id: str, options: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> Mapping[str, Any]:  # noqa: ARG002 - options reserved
evidence.note: FakeAdapter ignores options entirely (ARG002 suppression confirms). client_scope_requested is never checked.

evidence.snippet: def fetch_metrics(self, *, tenant_id: str, options: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> Mapping[str, Any]:
evidence.note: UploadAdapter returns TenantMetricsSnapshot payload without any platform/account filtering of rows.

evidence.snippet: def fetch_metrics(self, *, tenant_id: str, options: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> Mapping[str, Any]:
evidence.note: DemoAdapter reads start_date, end_date, parish, demo_tenant from options but not client_scoped_* keys.
meta_direct parses client_scoped_meta_ad_account_ids from options and filters on it (line 321). Google IDs field also silently ignored (no Google rows in this adapter).

findings.mitigation: For demo and fake adapters: implement a post-fetch row filter that slices campaign/creative/budget rows by client_scoped_meta_ad_account_ids when client_scope_requested is True, or return a 400 if scope keys arrive at an adapter that cannot honour them. For upload: same approach — rows in the CSV snapshot should be filtered by account_id list. Add parameterized pytest across all adapters in test_combined_platforms_only.py.

evidence.note: CampaignViewSet, AdSetViewSet, AdViewSet, RawPerformanceRecordViewSet all inherit TenantScopedModelViewSet — CRUD viewsets are now correctly scoped.
request.user.tenant used directly at two call sites (207, 312). If impersonation changes the tenant on the session but not on request.user.tenant, scoping diverges.
Single resolution point — takes tenant_id as str, not request.user.tenant. Consistent with the plan's 'single source of truth' requirement.

evidence.snippet: Analytics CRUD APIs | ... | Still using all_objects so the DRF base queryset ignores the tenant filter.
evidence.note: Audit doc marked this as fixed (TenantScopedModelViewSet) but the document still says 'Still using all_objects' — audit doc is stale.

findings.mitigation: Audit tenant_context_audit.md for staleness — TenantScopedModelViewSet appears to have superseded all_objects for CRUD views. Add an impersonation integration test asserting _collect_tenant_platform_accounts returns only the impersonated tenant's accounts. Verify request.user.tenant is set by the same middleware that sets tenant_id to ensure the two values are always in sync.

evidence.snippet: interface EmptyStateProps { icon: ReactNode; title: string; message: string; ... }
evidence.note: Shared EmptyState component exists and is used by Meta pages (MetaAccountsPage, MetaCampaignOverviewPage, MetaInsightsDashboardPage, PlatformDashboard, CampaignDashboard, AudienceDashboard, BudgetDashboard).

evidence.snippet: interface EmptyStateProps { icon, title, message, actionLabel?, onAction? }
evidence.note: No reason_code prop — callers pass free-text strings; plan calls for standard reason codes (no_accounts, no_data_for_range, adapter_error) but these are not enforced at the component level.
PlatformDashboard shows a StatusCard with variant=empty on error but not on status=loaded with 0 rows (silent blank chart risk).
Meta Insights correctly renders EmptyState for empty insights array. Pattern varies per page.

findings.mitigation: Add a reasonCode prop to EmptyState and a standard set of constants (no_accounts, no_data_for_range, adapter_error). Audit PlatformDashboard, CampaignDashboard, and AudienceDashboard to ensure status=loaded with 0 rows triggers EmptyState rather than a blank chart. Add a Vitest for each with a zero-row fixture.

evidence.note: resolveRoutePlatformScope is a private function inside DashboardLayout.tsx — no direct unit test exists for it in any test file.

evidence.snippet: it('deduplicates repeated dashboard error messages', ...) ... it('waits for Meta status before auto-selecting the preferred credential account', ...)
evidence.note: 10 tests in DashboardLayout.test.tsx; none assert platform scope behaviour (setFilters called with meta_ads or google_ads based on pathname).
GoogleAdsWorkspacePage.tsx has no matching __tests__ file in either routes/__tests__/ or routes/google-ads/__tests__/.
GoogleAdsWorkspacePage.test.tsx does exist in google-ads/__tests__ — but no test for workspace unified flag mode toggling.

evidence.snippet: Playwright spec files: campaigns, dashboard, export, health, map, meta-integration, meta-page-insights, meta-reporting-readiness, tenant-switch
evidence.note: No Playwright spec for /dashboards/google-ads (workspace), /dashboards/platforms, /dashboards/creatives, /dashboards/budget, /dashboards/audience, /dashboards/web/ga4, /dashboards/web/search-console.

findings.mitigation: Extract resolveRoutePlatformScope to a separate importable module (e.g. lib/routePlatformScope.ts) and add a table-driven Vitest covering all 20+ route prefixes. Add DashboardLayout integration tests asserting setFilters is called with the correct platforms array when mounted at /dashboards/meta/*, /dashboards/google-ads, and /dashboards/platforms. Add Playwright specs for missing routes.

evidence.snippet: export type MetaFilters = { accountId: string; campaignId: string; adsetId: string; ... };
evidence.note: useMetaStore has its own accountId in MetaFilters — entirely separate from useDashboardStore.filters.accountId.

useDashboardStore.filters does not include accountId at the type level (no accountId in FilterBarState grep results for DashboardStore), but DashboardLayout reads/writes filters.accountId at line 199 and 425.

evidence.snippet: const { filters, setFilters, accounts, insights } = useMetaStore((state) => ...)
evidence.note: MetaInsightsDashboardPage uses only useMetaStore.filters.accountId. Navigating from Meta Insights to /dashboards/platforms would show DashboardStore's accountId, which may differ.

evidence.snippet: const { accounts, filters, setFilters, loadAccounts } = useMetaStore(...)
evidence.note: MetaAccountsPage uses only useMetaStore — no sync back to useDashboardStore when user clicks an account row.

findings.mitigation: Define clear store ownership: useMetaStore.accountId is Meta workspace's local selection; useDashboardStore.filters.accountId is the global bar selection. Add a reconciliation effect in DashboardLayout that, when entering a /dashboards/meta/* route, mirrors useDashboardStore.filters.accountId into useMetaStore.filters.accountId (and vice versa on exit). Add a Vitest covering the round-trip.

evidence.note: Backend now emits two formats: 'platforms:<enabled>' (Sprint 10) and 'client:<id>' (Sprint 6).
Original client: prefix format.
apiClient.ts reads only content-type and content-disposition headers. X-Adinsights-Resolved-Via is never read in any frontend source file.
No frontend consumer of the header exists. The contract cannot regress if it is not consumed, but any future consumer will only know about the client: format.

findings.mitigation: Document the two-format contract (client:<id> and platforms:<list>) in a shared constants file and in the API response schema. Add a backend contract test asserting both formats are stable. When the frontend begins consuming the header (e.g. for debugging UI), parse both prefixes from the start.

open_questions: R1: Is there a window (between route change and the platform-scope useEffect firing) where useDashboardStore still holds the previous platform's campaign/creative rows and a child page renders them? Need a Playwright timing test to quantify the duration.

R3: The demo adapter is the adapter used in QA fixtures. If it silently ignores client_scoped_* keys, all QA tests that assert scoped data against the demo adapter are testing unscoped payloads — this could mask R1 in E2E.

R4: tenant_context_audit.md line 11 says CampaignViewSet 'Still using all_objects' but the current code uses TenantScopedModelViewSet with .objects. The audit doc appears stale. Verifiers should confirm whether the doc was not updated or whether there is a regression in the viewset.

R7: useDashboardStore.filters does not appear to have a top-level accountId field according to the store type definitions, yet DashboardLayout accesses filters.accountId at lines 199 and 425. This may rely on FilterBarState extending a different type — verifiers should confirm the runtime type of filters.accountId in useDashboardStore.

R6: GOOGLE_ADS_WORKSPACE_UNIFIED is resolved at module load time from an env var with default true. There is no test that flips the flag to false and exercises the legacy GoogleAdsExecutivePage / GoogleAdsCampaignsPage paths within the current test suite.
